title: Compiler-Derived Execution Conditioning Experiment
model: Gemma 4 12B (8-bit quantized, safetensors compiled to ComputeImage)

B_pipeline_warm.caveat: Conditioning runs on CPU (M=1 projection path). Backend equivalence to integrated GPU path not demonstrated. Treat as experimental.

C_rolling_prefetch.result: No measurable benefit — median decode worse than control. Page cache warming via cat segment_*.bin caused memory contention.

D_combined.result: Similar to Arm B alone — no emergent interaction benefit from prefetch. Conditioning dominates.

authorization_detail.pipeline_conditioning: PROMOTED — 16.4% median decode improvement exceeds 10% production threshold. Conditioning cost 152s, break-even ~20 requests. CPU substrate caveat: isolated M=1 replay runs on CPU while integrated layer uses GPU. Backend equivalence not physically demonstrated. Authorized for production integration with CPU-substrate disclosure.
authorization_detail.rolling_prefetch: REJECTED — no measurable integrated decode benefit. Page cache warming via fadvise caused memory pressure rather than latency reduction.
authorization_detail.combined_treatment: NO EMERGENT BENEFIT — Arm D performance within Arm B range. Prefetch adds overhead without decode improvement.
authorization_detail.custom_metal_kernel: NOT AUTHORIZED — pipeline conditioning achieved 16.4% improvement without custom kernels. No evidence quantized arithmetic kernel dominates integrated GPU path.

next_safe_action: Integrate pipeline conditioning into model-open lifecycle. Investigate GPU-path conditioning recipes for backend equivalence. Revisit bounded prefetch with per-layer madvise rather than full-model fadvise.
